context: Tuesday June 2 pre-market. Broad risk-off regime — Fear & Greed at 23 (EXTREME FEAR), down from the high-20s a week ago. Live tape: BTC $70,226 (-3.78% 24h / -8.42% 7d, leaking and sub-$73K supply), HBAR $0.0906 (-5.77% 24h but +3.39% 7d — the only green 7-day in the basket, ETF + enterprise tailwind underneath), XRP $1.27 (-3.08% / -5.56%, pinned below MA7/14/30 in a $1.26–$1.50 range), SUI $0.856 (-2.31% / -16.90%, basket-worst on network-reliability fears). The driver is macro, not crypto-idiosyncratic: a record BTC-ETF outflow streak (10 sessions, ~$2.97B — longest on record), oil >$105 on the Iran conflict keeping inflation expectations elevated, and a hawkish Fed (CME ~98% HOLD for the Jun 16-17 FOMC, 3.50–3.75%). Thinning liquidity into a heavy data week: JOLTS today, ADP + ISM Services Wed, Jobless Claims Thu, and NFP Friday Jun 5 (prior +115K, UR 4.3%) as the week's BINARY event. Cross-currents under the surface: XRP spot-ETF cumulative inflows hit a record ~$1.41B (diverging from BTC outflows) with CLARITY cleared Senate Banking 15-9 and a White House July-4 signing target; HBAR was SEC+CFTC-classified a 'digital commodity' (Mar 17) and its Canary spot ETF keeps absorbing supply. Trade plan: macro headwind dominates — BTC SHORT/NEUTRAL below $73K, HBAR NEUTRAL (only green-on-the-week, OTE long only on a $0.090 sweep + reclaim), XRP NEUTRAL range-trade, SUI SHORT on outage-driven weakness. NFP Friday is the event everything pivots on — keep size light until it clears.

tickers.thesis: XRP is pinned at $1.27 below all three daily MAs (MA7 ~$1.32 / MA14 ~$1.34 / MA30 ~$1.38), grinding the bottom of its $1.26–$1.50 range as the broad risk-off tape drags everything lower. The structural bull case is intact but dormant: CLARITY passed Senate Banking 15-9 on May 14 and the White House is targeting a July 4 signing, while cumulative spot-XRP-ETF inflows hit a record ~$1.41B — a notable relative-strength tell against BTC's record outflow streak. The problem is timing: there is NO near-term XRP-specific catalyst this week, so XRP trades as a macro-beta name into Friday's NFP. Plan: neutral-to-constructive at range lows. NO momentum shorts down here (you'd be selling into the $1.26 weekly demand); instead wait for a sweep of $1.26 + reclaim for an OTE long, or step aside until NFP clears. The line in the sand is a daily close below $1.26 — that opens $1.20, then the $1.12 cycle shelf. Hold $1.26 and a relief bounce can tag $1.34 mid-range.

tickers.thesis: BTC is the macro driver and it's leaking — $70,226 (-3.78% 24h, -8.42% 7d), pressed against the $70K round number after sliding from a $73,084 24h high. The bid is gone on every front the desks watch: a record 10-session ~$2.97B US spot-BTC-ETF outflow streak (longest ever), Coinbase premium soft (weak US demand), Kimchi premium below zero (no Korean bid), oil >$105 on the Iran conflict keeping inflation sticky, and a hawkish Fed locked ~98% to HOLD on June 16-17. This is a high-beta-macro tape, not digital gold. Bias SHORT/down: the path of least resistance is $70K → $67.5K unless buyers reclaim $73.5K. Tactics: NO new longs until a capitulation sweep + reclaim; fade relief rallies into the $73K broken-support supply. The whole week is gated by Friday's NFP — a clearly-soft print is the only near-term risk-on trigger; hot wages extend the bleed.

tickers.thesis: SUI is the basket's worst performer — $0.856 (-2.31% 24h, -16.9% 7d) — and it has an idiosyncratic problem on top of the macro tape: the chain HALTED PRODUCTION TWICE over May 29-30 (~9.5h total) after a critical gas-fee bug in the v1.72 update, sparking reliability concerns and an ~8% drop. That lands on top of an ongoing vesting schedule (~0.55% of supply unlocking per tranche) that keeps a steady sell bid. The institutional infrastructure is genuinely building — 21Shares TSUI on Nasdaq, Grayscale/Canary staking ETFs, CME SUI futures launched May 29, ~74% staked — but uptime risk now caps the multiple until the network proves stability. Bias SHORT: fade rallies into broken support; no longs until SUI both reclaims $0.95 AND strings together clean uptime. A break of $0.85 (24h low) opens the $0.80 round number, then $0.72. Only a network-stability + ETF-inflow combo flips the read.

tickers.thesis: HBAR is the basket's relative-strength name on the WEEK — +3.39% 7d is the only positive 7-day in the basket (vs BTC -8.42%, XRP -5.56%, SUI -16.9%) — but it still got dragged -5.77% on the day in the broad risk-off. The bull case is structural and real: HBAR was jointly classified a 'digital commodity' by the SEC + CFTC on Mar 17 2026, the Canary HBAR spot ETF (Nasdaq: HBR, live since Oct 28 2025) has absorbed ~549M HBAR (~1.3% of supply), and the enterprise pipeline keeps shipping — McLaren Racing just joined the Governing Council, and Lloyds/Aberdeen/Archax executed the UK's first FX trades using tokenized RWAs as collateral on Hedera. Offsets are macro, not idiosyncratic: this is a risk-off tape and HBAR has no momentum until BTC stabilizes. Bias NEUTRAL: range-bound $0.090–$0.11 with an ETF/enterprise tailwind underneath. Plan: long ONLY on a sweep of the $0.090 floor + reclaim (OTE), targeting $0.10 then the $0.11 breakout line; a daily close below $0.085 flips it bearish toward $0.080.

insights.body: Crypto Fear & Greed Index printed 23 (Extreme Fear) at 07:06Z — -6 vs yesterday's reading. F&G under 30 historically aligns with bounce-watch zones; under 20 with capitulation. Combined with the recent price action and live headlines (see LIVE FEED), the regime is risk-off until a sweep-and-reclaim candle prints. No new longs until F&G stops falling AND BTC reclaims its 24h high.

insights.title: NFP Friday Is the Week's Single Binary — Size Down Until It Clears
insights.body: The whole week funnels into Friday June 5's 8:30 AM ET payrolls print (prior +115K, unemployment 4.3%, plus Avg Hourly Earnings in the same release). The setup into it is fragile: F&G at 23 (Extreme Fear), a record 10-session BTC-ETF outflow streak (~$2.97B), oil >$105 on Iran keeping the Fed boxed in, and ~98% odds of a HOLD at the Jun 16-17 FOMC. JOLTS (today) and ADP (Wed) are the appetizers — a soft JOLTS builds the labour-cracks story and helps risk; a hot one extends the bleed. Trade plan: keep size light all week, no swing entries Thursday into Friday. A sub-100K NFP miss revives cut-odds and is the only clean risk-on trigger on the calendar; a hot beat (with hot wages) confirms the SHORT-BTC, range-XRP regime. Everything else is noise until 8:30 Fri.

insights.title: BTC's Record ETF Outflow Streak Is the Macro Driver — SHORT/NEUTRAL Below $73K
insights.body: BTC $70,226 (-3.78% 24h / -8.42% 7d) is leaking under a 10-session spot-ETF outflow streak of ~$2.97B — the longest on record. This is a flows story, not a structure story: oil >$105 (Iran) keeps inflation expectations elevated, the Fed is at ~98% HOLD, and thinning summer liquidity amplifies the down-moves. Bias is SHORT/NEUTRAL while price sits below the $73K supply shelf — that's the level that has to reclaim with volume before any long thesis is valid. Confirming tells under the surface: Kimchi premium below zero (no Korean retail bid) and a weak Coinbase premium (soft US institutional demand). Don't bottom-pick into NFP; let the outflow streak break first.

insights.title: HBAR Is the Only Green-on-the-Week Name — NEUTRAL With a Structural Tailwind
insights.body: HBAR $0.0906 is -5.77% on the day in the risk-off, but +3.39% on the WEEK — the only positive 7-day in the basket (vs BTC -8.42%, XRP -5.56%, SUI -16.9%). The bull case is structural: SEC+CFTC classified HBAR a 'digital commodity' (Mar 17 2026), the Canary spot ETF (Nasdaq: HBR) has absorbed ~549M HBAR (~1.3% of supply), and the enterprise pipeline keeps shipping — McLaren Racing joined the Governing Council and Lloyds/Aberdeen/Archax ran the UK's first tokenized-RWA-collateral FX trades on Hedera. Bias is NEUTRAL: no momentum until BTC stabilizes, range $0.090–$0.11. The only long worth taking is an OTE buy on a clean $0.090 sweep + reclaim, targeting $0.10 then the $0.11 breakout; otherwise stand aside. If BTC's outflow streak breaks and risk turns, the ETF/enterprise bid is the edge.

insights.title: XRP — The Quiet Relative-Strength Story Hiding Under a Range
insights.body: XRP $1.27 (-3.08% / -5.56%) looks weak on price — pinned below MA7/14/30 in a $1.26–$1.50 range — but the flows tell the opposite story: spot-XRP-ETF cumulative inflows hit a record ~$1.41B, diverging hard from BTC's record outflows. CLARITY cleared Senate Banking 15-9 (May 14) and the White House is targeting a July 4 signing. Bias is NEUTRAL/HIGH: this is a range-trade, not a momentum trade — buy the $1.26 lower bound with a tight stop, fade the $1.45-$1.50 upper bound, and only flip directional on a clean break of either. The ETF-inflow vs BTC-outflow divergence is the relative-strength edge to watch if the whole basket turns up post-NFP.

insights.title: SUI Is a Structural-Risk SHORT, Not Just a Price SHORT
insights.body: SUI $0.856 (-2.31% 24h / -16.90% 7d) is the basket-worst, and the reason matters: two network outages over May 29-30 (~9.5h of downtime combined) put reliability fears front and centre — that's a structural risk, not a dip to buy. Ongoing vesting unlocks add steady sell pressure. There are offsets (21Shares TSUI, CME SUI futures since May 29, staking ETFs) but they don't fix the uptime narrative in the near term. Bias is SHORT/MEDIUM: trade with the outage-driven weakness, target continuation while price holds below the broken structure. The invalidation is a sustained reclaim plus a clean multi-day stretch with no further downtime headlines.

insights.title: Hidden Divergence — Crypto-ETF Flows Are Splitting the Basket
insights.body: The standout cross-current this week is that ETF flows are no longer moving as one bloc. BTC is in a record 10-session outflow streak (~$2.97B) while XRP spot ETFs just hit a record ~$1.41B cumulative inflow and HBAR's Canary spot ETF (HBR) keeps absorbing supply (~549M HBAR, ~1.3% of float) on the back of the SEC+CFTC digital-commodity classification. That split is why the basket isn't trading as a single risk-on/risk-off unit — BTC and SUI are the weak legs (macro + outages), XRP and HBAR are the relative-strength legs (regulatory + ETF/enterprise bid). The actionable read: if NFP triggers a turn, lead with HBAR/XRP, not BTC; if it confirms risk-off, BTC and SUI are the cleaner shorts.

hidden_signals.body: Fear & Greed at 23 (Extreme Fear), Δ -6 vs yesterday — sub-30 zones historically align with bounce setups but require a sweep-and-reclaim candle to confirm
HackerNews trending crypto-tagged: "Palindromes by Eric Harshbarger" (↑12)

BTC spot-ETF outflow streak hit 10 consecutive sessions (~$2.97B cumulative) — the longest on record; this is the mechanical driver of the -8.42% weekly bleed, not a structure break. The tell to watch is the FIRST net-inflow day — that, not a price candle, marks the flows turn

Kimchi premium is BELOW ZERO — Korean retail is not bidding; combined with the ETF outflows this confirms there is no retail or institutional sponsor under BTC right now. A flip back positive would be the earliest Asian-session sign of a demand return

Coinbase premium is weak/negative — soft US institutional demand on the spot book; together with (a) and (b) all three demand gauges (ETF, Korean retail, US institutional) point the same direction, which is why the down-moves are running with so little resistance

Oil >$105 on the Iran conflict is keeping inflation expectations elevated — this is the macro tail that boxes the Fed into a HOLD (~98% for Jun 16-17) and indirectly caps every risk-asset bounce; an oil de-escalation headline is an underpriced risk-ON catalyst

HBAR is +3.39% on the WEEK while the rest of the basket is red — the only green 7-day; its Canary spot ETF (HBR) keeps absorbing supply (~549M HBAR) on the SEC+CFTC digital-commodity classification — a structural bid hiding under a -5.77% risk-off day. Basket-leader candidate if risk turns

XRP spot-ETF cumulative inflows hit a record ~$1.41B — diverging hard from BTC's record outflows; the basket's ETF flows are splitting, not moving as one bloc. This relative-strength tell is invisible on the price chart (XRP -3% on the day) but is the cleaner long if NFP turns risk-on

SUI's two network outages over May 29-30 (~9.5h combined downtime) are a STRUCTURAL reliability risk, not a price dip — the -16.9% weekly drop is the market repricing uptime risk, compounded by ongoing vesting unlocks. Treat reliability headlines, not price, as the SUI invalidation/confirm
